es.title: Noticias Diarias: Copyright e Inteligencia Artificial
es.subtitle: Lo último en leyes de derechos de autor para músicos, compositores y productores.

sections.title: Cambios Recientes en la Ley de Copyright
sections.content: La Oficina de Derechos de Autor de los Estados Unidos ha establecido que las obras creadas únicamente por inteligencia artificial no pueden ser registradas, ya que carecen de autoría humana. Sin embargo, si un ser humano ha intervenido significativamente en la creación de la obra, puede ser elegible para el registro. Esto se debe a que la ley de derechos de autor requiere que la obra sea creada por un ser humano para ser considerada elegible para la protección.

sections.title: ¿Qué se puede y qué no se puede registrar?
sections.content: Los músicos pueden registrar sus obras si han utilizado inteligencia artificial como herramienta, siempre y cuando hayan intervenido significativamente en la creación de la obra. Por ejemplo, si un productor utiliza un software de inteligencia artificial para generar un ritmo, pero luego edita y personaliza el ritmo, puede registrar la obra. Sin embargo, si el software de inteligencia artificial crea la obra sin intervención humana, no puede ser registrada.

sections.title: Productores: Edición vs Intervención Humana
sections.content: Si un productor agrega instrumentos reales o edita una pista generada por inteligencia artificial, puede registrar la obra si la intervención humana es significativa. La clave es determinar si la intervención humana ha agregado un elemento creativo y original a la obra. Si la intervención humana se limita a ajustar parámetros o hacer ediciones menores, es posible que no sea suficiente para considerar la obra como una creación humana original.

en.title: Daily News: Copyright & Artificial Intelligence
en.subtitle: The latest in copyright law for musicians, composers, and producers.

sections.title: Recent Changes in Copyright Law
sections.content: The United States Copyright Office has established that works created solely by artificial intelligence cannot be registered, as they lack human authorship. However, if a human has significantly contributed to the creation of the work, it may be eligible for registration. This is because copyright law requires that the work be created by a human to be considered eligible for protection. The US Copyright Office has recently clarified that it will not register works produced by artificial intelligence without human intervention, citing the need for human authorship.

sections.title: What Can and Cannot Be Registered?
sections.content: Musicians can register their works if they have used artificial intelligence as a tool, as long as they have significantly contributed to the creation of the work. For example, if a producer uses artificial intelligence software to generate a beat, but then edits and customizes the beat, they can register the work. However, if the artificial intelligence software creates the work without human intervention, it cannot be registered. The key is to determine whether the human contribution has added a creative and original element to the work.

sections.title: Producers: Editing vs. Human Intervention
sections.content: If a producer adds real instruments or edits an artificial intelligence-generated track, they can register the work if the human intervention is significant. The crucial factor is determining whether the human intervention has added a creative and original element to the work. If the human intervention is limited to adjusting parameters or making minor edits, it may not be sufficient to consider the work as an original human creation. The US Copyright Office has emphasized that the level of human intervention required for registration will depend on the specific circumstances of each case.
